---
name: Domain Supplement Proposal
about: Propose a new industry-specific supplement
title: "[DOMAIN] "
labels: domain-supplement, enhancement
assignees: ''
---

## Domain

[E.g., medical, finance, gaming, education, legal, real estate, etc.]

## Why this needs its own supplement

Why isn't `general.md` enough? What's domain-specific?

Examples of good reasons:
- Heavy domain jargon with established UA equivalents
- Tension between casual industry talk and formal/regulatory language
- Anglicism patterns specific to this industry
- Common AI mistakes specific to this content

## Scope

What content types would this supplement cover?

- [ ] Customer-facing content (marketing, support, UX)
- [ ] Internal team content
- [ ] Regulatory / compliance documents
- [ ] Sales / B2B content
- [ ] Technical documentation
- [ ] Other: ____________

## Are you willing to maintain it?

- [ ] Yes, I'll write the initial version and maintain
- [ ] I'll write it but need help maintaining
- [ ] I'm proposing it but someone else needs to write it
- [ ] I can review/contribute but not lead

## Your qualifications

Optional but helpful: how do you know this domain? (E.g., "I work in [domain]," "I've localized [domain] content for X years," etc.)

## Initial scope sketch

If you're going to write it, sketch what terminology areas it would cover. This helps reviewers gauge effort and scope.

Example for medical:
- Specialty names (cardiology, oncology, etc.)
- Procedure names
- Anatomy
- Patient communication register (vs medical professional register)
- Regulatory terms (FDA equivalents, MOH directives)
- Pharmaceutical naming
